title: AR Trait
description: A trait is a named set of structural requirements that a record type can declare conformance to. Traits are composition-based: a record type 'satisfies' a trait by meeting its requirements, not by inheriting. AR's standard trait library is derived from IR's 6 families and 9 profile kinds, but custom traits may be defined by any domain.

derives_from.description: Upstream identifiers (typically IR.* for AR-shipped traits, or SE.* for SE-derived traits).

ir.description: If the trait is derived from an IR profile, declare which one. AR's standard trait library uses this field; user-defined domain traits may omit it.

ir_version.description: Version of the IR vocabulary this trait is derived from. AR ships a pinned IR snapshot.

requirements.description: Structural requirements a record type must satisfy to claim conformance to this trait. Each is independently verifiable.

must_have_fields.description: Field names the record type must declare in every record of that type.

must_not_have_fields.description: Field names the record type must NOT carry. Their presence is a collapse signal.

must_not_collapse_with.description: Trait IDs whose simultaneous conformance violates IR non-collapse. Verifier reports cannot-verify when collapsing trait is not declared elsewhere.

transformation_limits.description: IR transformation identifiers (from IR's 10-family basis) that records of this type MAY undergo. Others are forbidden.

narrative.description: Human-readable explanation. Useful for implementors who don't want to learn IR theory. Read this; satisfy the requirements; move on.
